name: Team Kasich
subject: Thank You Veterans & Upcoming Interviews
body:
Our team would like to take a moment to thank America's veterans
for your service and sacrifice to defend our freedoms.

Peter, our team would like to
take a moment to thank America's veterans for your service and
sacrifice to defend our freedoms.

If you know any veterans, please
join us and take a moment to reach out to them this weekend to
express your thanks.

We also wanted to let you know
that Gov. John Kasich will be a guest on CNN's "State of the
Union" and ABC's "This Week" this upcoming Sunday, Nov. 12th.
Find details below about how you can tune in:

CNN: 9:00AM & Noon ET

ABC: Find your local listing
(here)

Thank you for all that you're
doing to help.

-Team Kasich

- Veterans Affairs Secretary David Shulkin in The Hill

- Bloomberg

President Trump's Administration has shifted U.S.-Cuba policy to "move toward greater political and economic freedom for the Cuban people."

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=fbf5654851e33239e7f02a2667d0bc2a4ab76b88d59d3752719a9528840479c0abe1b3a09c736d3937f5cda5f69d9fed4580cfe0b98dd94c

- Reuters

Following the President's leadership on Venezuela, the European Union is enacting its own arms embargo on the dictatorship.

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: This Veterans Day
body:
|
---
| | Hello --

Today is a day to recognize and honor our nation's veterans.

There are over 1.5 million veterans living here in Florida and the sacrifices they've made in order to protect us and our families deserve more thanks than we'll ever be able to give them. It is on all of us to ensure that the brave men and women who serve in our military come home to a grateful nation and have the support and resources they need.

In 2012, Tallahassee became the first Capital Purple Heart City in the nation. From offering veterans free transportation to and from the VA Clinic, to honoring our Purple Heart recipients every year, I've been proud to continue and build on our City's commitment to our veterans.

And, as Governor, I will continue to do that by ensuring physical and mental health care and job-training opportunities are available for all of our veterans. So, while today is set aside as a special day to honor and recognize all of the sacrifices that veterans have made for us, we must continue to fight to make every day like Veterans Day. So, let's start today.

**[Add your name and join me in thanking the Veterans in our communities. It may seem like a simple thing to do, but it means a lot.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3274/9e00690/4eb4ee39/24031eab/3115259041/VEsH/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/thank-a-veteran?utm_campaign=em17_171111_Vets_acq_gq_ful__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em17_171111_Vets_acq_gq_ful__AG&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+and+join+me+in+thanking+th")**

Happy Veterans Day,

Andrew

name: Team Donnelly
subject: He'll never stop fighting for veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
|
---

| | Peter,

Today is Veterans Day, a day that serves to honor all sacrifices that our servicemen and women have made to defend our great nation. Traveling around the state, Joe has heard so many stories from our veterans and their families about their service and the issues they are facing. **That 's why he's always been such a strong leader on veterans' issues in Congress.**

From working to open a state-of-the-art V.A. Medical Center in Michiana, to reforming the VA so that all veterans have immediate access to the best health care, to seeing legislation enacted that guarantees for the first time ever that every single veteran and active duty service member receives annual mental health assessments to ensure they're receiving the care they need, Joe is a true advocate for the men and women who have worn the uniform of our country.

**[Show Joe today that you support his work looking out for our veterans. Sign up here to let Joe know that you've got his back when he's got theirs.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2569/7377f838/353183dd/51fba801/365869142/VEsH/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/add-your-name-to-stand-with-joe-veterans")** | [](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2569/7377f838/353183dd/51fba801/365869142/VEsE/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/add-your-name-to-stand-with-joe-veterans")

**Peter, being able to give back to those who have served our nation is one of the most important things Joe has done while in Congress.** He has repeatedly reached across the aisle to pass legislation that helps our nation's veterans. And he has no plans to stop.

**[Sign up today: Make sure one of our biggest champions for veterans stays in the Senate.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2569/7377f838/353183dd/51fba801/365869142/VEsF/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/add-your-name-to-stand-with-joe-veterans")**

Thanks,
Team Donnelly

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: keeping our promises
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
This weekend we honor the service of our veterans; men and women who stepped up to serve their country with pride and bravery.

They answered the call -- and America is a better nation for their lasting commitment.

But words of gratitude must be backed by direct action. A grateful nation must support those who are willing to risk their lives for our safety and freedom.

Caring for our veterans and their families should never be a partisan, political issue.

No matter our political beliefs, we can work together, and provide our veterans with quality health care, mental health services, education benefits, affordable housing, and the opportunity to succeed when they return back home.

As we honor our veterans, let us all double and redouble our efforts to ensure our nation keeps its promises to all who have served -- and to their families that count on us to be there for them.

With gratitude,

Chris Van Hollen

U.S. Senator

name: Team Ryan
subject: Thank you, veterans!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Ryan, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Ryan for Congress, Inc
body:
Friend,

Across the country, something happens when Americans see our flag raised. When our anthem is played.** ** When our veterans salute.****

For just a moment, children stand, women put their hands over their hearts, men take off their hats . . . for just one moment, we are united. United in national pride.

Today, let's put aside politics. Instead, **let 's thank all of our veterans who have fought and continue to fight for freedom.** They have made America the greatest country in the world.

Thank you,

Team Ryan

name: Team Leah
subject: Leah's Weekly Roundup 11/11/2017 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Leah for Senate
body:
Today we honor those who have worn the uniform to uphold our Constitution and defend our freedoms. There will never be enough that we can do to.......
*|MC_PREVIEW_TEXT|* | | |
---
| | | [ ](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=ee40075554b24653aa5ba9abef1c9e2d&_e=-iMiNvRNnH7Lb30bvOIvlkalv6cO6sELklSw2CoOsLl7beNPCwZPGxwtSBLBu178sdnxEec-7AWNnqrln0kSedbR8r2r80t3ujy8iMSfghx6-qBocJ0hWlx_i_NyhF-Gnhf_QHOJVr2L1kOIMHTdDUybzZQ-m9Q73DBuiilxUtzQx0jPnws98f-9cCP9jhrPIWk-JsPziUnyE89Wzj7y3dVEyqcBuNcjFzl2lKNKAFc0jIyIXEST8oCtbV4Ic9wV)
---
| | | Nov. 11, 2017

**Happy Veterans Day!**

Today we honor those who have worn the uniform to uphold our Constitution and defend our freedoms. There will never be enough that we can do to thank our veterans for the sacrifices they have made for us. [Leah is challenging everyone to chat with a veteran this weekend](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=ee40075554b24653aa5ba9abef1c9e2d&_e=LTmvRBxHHULe4S4yn_SmkkZoNOQsYzi3Ebm6_28maPN358wtCvXO632xlg-yE3N844u4gi3pSoHjeqPAzA22m9kvtSbz8hBgpQag6ld9E_NB7KrDO8ke5zFD0UNdscZP_szTnj3aXLBJuPSaS27Ys_EBxed3mYp-PtwV30NBRREZmftnEL3tcqmmEHEDaqlVspnNYL46YGmizlQ7QTTs45vP_604tDJvdPWpZDoDHAQQitqYS2kUk7fYA_tL5Hp-PGPINJyxemTfDK_sdohDxpOMa2rJVadBJh_KIOCULVo%3D), thank them for their service, and ask them to share a story or two.

On Tuesday, seven of Leah's bills passed the state Senate. One of the bills, which now heads to the governor's desk, ensures that our veterans will be reimbursed for their full tuition costs and guarantees [that the state keeps our promises to our veterans.](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=ee40075554b24653aa5ba9abef1c9e2d&_e=LTmvRBxHHULe4S4yn_SmkpNxMWUnGns-TCzli9M640VFuwBeKKhX-hgJ-_M0k7Bu7BhgDAuLE1lit52zzmQpsUWqtct9Y4kq7Cxz45o1z8PX-KlIuH7JAZhC3PfXmdnq_gkZk2OmnaBjH5je_65-ZRuKu6RPRvXBp-yM6igq8KY2q2zdw4BskOmn7d6kx_PFX9aU4JMyIZFNlRFKsXGocZRAaTslwUXBn-K5H3xAHXGBXxw1qFk0eJm08BeMQeFF3O2WN8bxrZaAetd2cXKC11df_Vd_d2YtcLaRULhq0WgThtFPwsiDSTgoxMg7crWk)

Five of Leah's bills that passed focused on [making sure that repeat violent criminals are not terrorizing our communities.](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=ee40075554b24653aa5ba9abef1c9e2d&_e=JsJ2ey0aDk_6fwvffziluusncFLH8FcEpbuaV_itx8UoXR4I-6Q9BGjX--rE9D0OywTwa12Edf5oQUgNQqZhgEJY0W9RUGsUeWIM72nxhEbT5Zn5qXlNcTtq7tsVr5OD6cQ3upl3ApQPdL5M0l-EmMtRA6PO7JntMi1lUFPjrQgIlpy9iVx4BXYA0A3OjnoSU77hYN58207upFpArKMtdpCihxAzAvM8KXOLEWfbvde10QbxjYT8zn6iJfEB616X0IIBIfM-K3EIqeY0bwK_0Di54-nZ8H70loYdiw6ZhMQt0Pn0TgekhuHqS9vf_5j_zKMPx8yQ-vyKWMvRgjKgnD69lpTIedhwcrEJbCq6S_GSTQ1zaJKhLS5YKAPgSUGOhLgNEoOTw5Yj63jm2QBogQ%3D%3D) This includes a bill to require the Department of Corrections to recommend revoking probation for already convicted criminals who are charged with another felony or violent misdemeanor.

[This week reinforced that Leah is the only clear, consistent conservative](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=ee40075554b24653aa5ba9abef1c9e2d&_e=owKHPo7Pe_yDzqaJ6PfDzPbqFaR2BKsRCjgz67AO79w7M8kb_KfcJJBguknFpmAj7S7Nq-OaPlEtcpb9JT4hEObha2CvYWfOqpAwdkDew2Rc945h_O19ImG7eIRRUoBH6xaK1gRNm7xZr1QQX_Bv7oxL-q0svK9wimgGlBfNQnEdn2k1VOwGPbietl9k-plTm4Nq8KqaDhjuYdImqWPGjDThJWqQaZloPNz6qrkEl5YBdRXbWvyOOW8k8wQ31e11G0SJsLBfLOWa6FeR0uwLDzBHlU-nCShpBxGLg00JnM9Mmi2njg-mWRaVaYGVO1ZXR-oDF3U7s_zc-6aP4MEXn-Rxjmnx1me1eIierXC9pxgTrxBykwJxuqPE7w6r7Vb4) in the U.S. Senate race. Leah's proven record on Second Amendment issues is why we can trust her to defend our right to bear arms in the U.S. Senate. She has an A rating from the National Rifle Association and will continue her work to ensure law-abiding citizens can enjoy these freedoms while getting guns out of the hands of criminals.

Earlier this week, [Leah joined Scott Walker and Rebecca Kleefisch](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=ee40075554b24653aa5ba9abef1c9e2d&_e=LTmvRBxHHULe4S4yn_SmkvaQQrPxW621HbdDp4tBMyjb8WOl2RTCjp6OPMamgYGQVvdnn5BhI6yrpkT-1UiGb3zMdf5E_iertOEADXLAKRtf0pk-20tc7GyNS6EKM6ltVnqU3xO1F6NPW0Ru0oyLG9_ZlkagCussZv_WHERmMU0QeYhmCngxy3WiUw7ghW1mrXPeBJX1rS2K3TZDoW4xlk_hY78xLsuJLH1okc66YFjqD5FdPQKURBtQXdktqt7tTOheTnML21aTexJgZ60rZzI3lKTfCzXXlGWsNL9Bw0rSyb8ZXeYXirHlrOXpdPSeljJGcPgIOUwXDfa_gNPBVW6BPrPu4E5upzRUqGCkpPI%3D) for their re-election campaign kickoff. We cannot take their leadership for granted, and it will be important for us all to work together to ensure victories in November for these proven conservative reformers.

Leah's been continuing to tour the state. name: Roy Cooper
subject: Thank you will never be enough
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
Our veterans honor us with their service and their sacrifices, and it's fitting that we honor them.
|

---

Friend,

Our veterans honor us with their service and their sacrifices, and it's fitting that we honor them.

Veterans Day is an important reminder of all that veterans have done to make our state and our nation stronger.

North Carolina is proud to be the home of so many men and women who served bravely in our armed forces. One in ten North Carolinians are veterans, and we have the third largest active military population in the country.

We owe these veterans and servicemembers our gratitude for protecting our safety and preserving our freedoms.

We also owe them the opportunity to make a life for themselves when their military service ends. That includes help transitioning into civilian careers and access to health care and other support they've earned as veterans.

I'm proud that North Carolina is one of the most military friendly states in the country, and the home to so many distinguished veterans.

**To our veterans: a simple thank you will never be enough. You put yourselves in harm 's way to protect our freedoms and our way of life.
